claim: On September 12, 2025, Tyler James Robinson, 22, was charged in Utah County with aggravated murder, felony discharge of a firearm, two counts of obstruction of justice, two counts of witness tampering, three counts of victim-targeting enhancement, and violence committed in the presence of a child. The recovered murder weapon was a bolt-action .30-06 rifle found near the scene; on September 15, 2025 the FBI announced that Robinson's DNA matched DNA on the rifle's trigger, on a towel wrapped around the rifle, and on a screwdriver recovered from the roof of the firing position. Utah prosecutors announced they would seek the death penalty.

sources.label: Utah County Attorney — Charging Information (TJR), State of Utah v. Tyler James Robinson
Wikipedia — “Assassination of Charlie Kirk”
sources.quote: matched that found on a towel that was wrapped around the suspected rifle

sources.label: PBS NewsHour — “As officials searched for Charlie Kirk's shooter, suspect confessed to his partner, prosecutor says”
CNN — “Tyler Robinson: What we know about Kirk shooting suspect” (Sep 12, 2025)
BIBLE Companion / @bible.app_, Instagram reel DTQb5oiAMHb (Jan 8, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: Anchored to y2025. The Utah County Attorney's charging document (PDF) is the primary court record; Wikipedia URL fetched and verbatim DNA-match phrasing confirmed. The reel disputes that Robinson committed the killing (claims an exploding lavalier microphone was the actual mechanism, triggered by a 'palm gun guy in brown shirt'). The official forensic record — long-range rifle, DNA match on trigger and towel, charging-document — is unambiguous on the prosecution's theory. Whether that theory survives trial is open; this record documents the charges as filed.
